original_image_tags: Scan of the reverse side of a pink-colored document page. The page shows signs of age and wear, with ragged, torn edges on the right side and along the top. Two hole-punch marks are visible at the top. The paper is pink, which in this archival context typically denotes a "SECRET" classification level. Faint, reversed ghosting of typed text from the front side of the document is visible through the paper. The bottom of the page is heavily obscured by brown adhesive tape, likely archival or repair tape. The page appears to be a routing slip or attachment cover sheet.

elements.description: Pink paper back of a page with multiple stamps and tape. Two circular punch holes are visible at the top. A faded inverted stamp at the top reads 'AUG 2 1947'. A blue stamp near the bottom left reads 'RECEIVED-TAMM FBI U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE AUG 13 9 39 AM 47'. An inverted blue stamp near the bottom left reads 'INTERNAL SECURITY F.B.I. 25 3 53 PM'. Two pieces of brown tape with illegible text are attached to the left and right edges.
